issues.problem: Primary message claims 'smudge-proof' brows, but reviews indicate product is not smudge-proof alone.
issues.example: That alone won't make it smudge proof, but if you also use The Pore-fessional - Agent Zero Shine by Benefit, this will help keep it smudge proof...

recommendations: Adjust primary messaging to clarify product is not inherently smudge-proof but works well with complementary products.
Include more explicit evidence or testimonials about the value of included tools (brush and tweezers).
Expand on competitor comparisons to strengthen differentiation.
Monitor customer feedback to validate assumptions about prioritizing affordability and natural results.

keep: Affordable, effective brow kit with natural-looking results
Use of real customer reviews as evidence
Target audience focus on sparse brows and scar coverage
change: Primary message to remove or qualify 'smudge-proof' claim
Highlight compatibility with additional products rather than implying standalone smudge-proofness
